pages.title: About | Daniel Francis MP
pages.text_excerpt: About | Daniel Francis MP Find me on Facebook 0207 219 5278 HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS NEWSLETTERS USEFUL LINKS Useful Links Advice & Support Legislative Update: Key Government Bills HELP Contact Visit Parliament Event Request ABOUT Find out more about my work, campaigns and how to help make our local area better for everyone. ABOUT ME I delivered my Maiden Speech in Parliament in September 2024 as the new MP for Bexleyheath and Crayford. It was only right to start by paying tribute to my predecessor Sir David Evennett, who served as the MP since 2005, and before that as MP for Erith and Crayford 1983-1997. I’m proud to have spent my whole life living in our borough. I’ve devoted 20 years to local public service as a councillor representing Belvedere. I’ve held many roles in my time on the council including four years as Leader of the Opposition. I served as Cabinet Member for Transport under the last Labour administration from 2003-06 where I worked with trade unions to save direct Bexleyheath to Victoria train services, delivered road safety improvements, and ensured the Elizabeth Line came south of the river to Abbey Wood. I joined the Labour Party thirty years ago committed to building investment in public services and an economy that delivers for working people. I saw the difference the last Labour Government made to our public services and have watched in despair at how the Conservatives have crashed the economy and decimated our public services. It is a privilege to have been elected as the Labour MP for Bexleyheath and Crayford in July 2024 as part of the new Labour Government. It’s an opportunity for change and about giving the people of Bexleyheath and Crayford their future park. My wife is a teacher in the borough and we have two primary aged children – one of which has cerebral palsy and requires full time care support. I work closely with local parent groups supporting and campaigning on behalf of children with disabilities and their families both across the borough and nationally with SCOPE and the Changing Places campaign. While the Conservatives don’t seem to appreciate how tough things are for local families, I understand the struggles faced across the constituency. As a family, we know how difficult the cost of living crisis and the sharp rise in mortgage and rents after the Conservatives crashed the economy has made things. With Keir Starmer at the helm, Labour is back in the service of working people. We will put an end to 14 years of Tory chaos and decline with a plan for national renewal, built on a bedrock of economic stability. Our Labour Government is delivering the change we need to take back our streets, get the NHS back on its feet, break down barriers to opportunity and get Britain building again. MY PRIORITIES I have lived in this area my whole life. I know the challenges our area and our community faces. I will always be a strong local voice in Parliament, championing local issues and fighting on your behalf. As your MP, I work tirelessly all year round to make sure that I can effectively represent you. I host regular drop-in surgeries to support constituents. I know I won’t please everyone all of the time. But as your local representative, I always do what I think best represents the views of our area and make it clear why I have come to a particular position. Housing for all I believe that housing is a universal human right. As a local councillor for 20 years, I saw first hand how badly we need to improve the standard of housing and provide more affordable housing. Working with housing associations in the constituency, I am working to improve decent home standards and find ways to maximise the provision of affordable housing on new development sites. The growth of street homelessness in the constituency is a disgrace and I continue to support our Labour Government to assist those impacted and to reduce those living on our streets. pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns | Daniel Francis MP Find me on Facebook 0207 219 5278 HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS NEWSLETTERS USEFUL LINKS Useful Links Advice & Support Legislative Update: Key Government Bills HELP Contact Visit Parliament Event Request CAMPAIGNS Click on the links below to find out more about local campaigns to make our local area a fairer and safer place for all. Facebook X-twitter Instagram Envelope COMMUNITY CENTRES local campaign Local families have lost a vital hub since Parkside Community Centre was knocked down by the council. We cannot let this space be lost forever. Complete my survey today, I want to hear from local residents about the future of Parkside Community Centre. Details SLADE GREEN LIGHTS local campaign Residents have reported feeling unsafe using the bridge after dark on the footbridge at Slade Green. Your local MP has been working with Slade Green & Northend ward councillors to get Network Rail to reinstate lighting on Slade Green’s footbridge after its wrongful removal. Details POTHOLE PATROL local campaign The Pothole Patrol campaign started in the summer of 2025, and ended up going all the way to Westminster. Every road in the constituency was checked and all potholes were measured and reported to the council. Resulting in increased spend of the council's road resurfacing budget. Details GET IN TOUCH As your local MP there are a number of different things I can help you with or offer advice on, such as; benefits, immigration, tax issues, problems with the NHS, child maintenance and education matters. pages.title: Parkside survey - Daniel Francis MP | Bexleyheath and Crayford
pages.text_excerpt: Parkside survey - Daniel Francis MP | Bexleyheath and Crayford Find me on Facebook 0207 219 5278 HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS NEWSLETTERS USEFUL LINKS Useful Links Advice & Support Legislative Update: Key Government Bills HELP Contact Visit Parliament Event Request Home » Parkside survey Parkside survey 01/05/2026 Posted by: Daniel Francis MP Category: News No Comments Share on Social Media Parkside Community Centre: What Local Residents Told Me Last year, Parkside Community Centre in Barnehurst was demolished by the London Borough of Bexley, leaving families, community groups and local residents without a vital shared space. Parkside was more than just a building. It hosted a nursery, youth groups, music lessons, fitness classes, community events and served as a polling station. For many families – including my own – it was part of everyday life. Since its demolition, the site has remained empty and unused. Bexley Council has said it wants the site to remain in community use – but only if a charitable organisation can fund and build a new centre themselves. This approach puts all the financial risk on local groups, many of whom are already stretched. It creates a real danger that no organisation will be able to take this on, and that Parkside is lost forever. If the council is genuine about keeping this site for the community, it must do more. I Asked Local Residents for Their Views To make sure residents’ voices were heard, my office launched a survey of people who live locally or previously used Parkside Community Centre. Local residents responded, and the message was clear: of respondents said their household had previously used Parkside Community Centre said the centre was important or very important to their household said the council itself should rebuild the community centre said they would oppose the site being sold to developers 70% were unaware that the council had no plans to rebuild the centre itself Residents highlighted the loss of: childcare and family activities spaces for young people opportunities for older residents local hub within walking distance Barnehurst now has no community centre, with the nearest alternatives over 20 minutes away on foot or by public transport. Survey respondents were clear about the future they want for Parkside: like‑for‑like community centre, rebuilt on the existing site for families, children and young people to support health, wellbeing and reducing loneliness welcoming, inclusive hub that brings neighbours together Above all, residents want the council to take responsibility and ensure that Parkside remains a community asset. What Happens Next I have shared these findings publicly and raised the issue in Parliament to highlight the wider impact of losing community spaces like Parkside. I will continue pressing Bexley Council on its current approach to rebuilding Parkside Community Centre to ensure this site for community use in the long term Powered By EmbedPress Share on Social Media KEEP UPDATED SUBSCRIBE FOR EMAIL UPDATES Keep Updated Subscribe to my email newsletter to keep updated on our news, campaigns and work in the local area.
